executive_summary: Your amber status is driven entirely by supplier concentration risk, not macro headwinds or peer issues. Two distinct clusters demand immediate attention: China-exposed suppliers (IP Sun, Porton, Huizhou BYD, Smoore, EVE Energy) face unified US-China trade and Taiwan escalation risk across EMS, batteries, and nicotine—this is a single failure mode affecting multiple categories. Separately, your two largest stock crashes are unexplained declines at Stora Enso (Finland, printing substrates, -11.4%) and Infineon (Germany, EE components, -5.7%), both critical-risk rated; investigate these immediately as they may signal operational or demand issues beyond geopolitics. The Sappi decline and geopolitical flags on India (ITC) and South Korea (Tae Young) are secondary—monitor but not urgent. Action now: (1) get root cause confirmation on Stora Enso and Infineon within 48 hours, (2) stress-test your China supply chain for Taiwan-scenario impact and identify single non-China alternatives for EMS and batteries, (3) defer deeper South Africa and India review to next cycle.
